meta.hypothesis: Symmetry check + 2nd shot: re-confirm BOTH MES/MNQ divergence lead directions (MES-leads->trade MNQ AND MNQ-leads->trade MES) head-to-head, and apply a causal concentration fix (as-of VIX regime gate x day-trend-side alignment) to the MES->MNQ lead to try to clear gate 5 (drop-top5 per-trade > 0) -- the gate the B4 divergence lead failed. Futures point-P&L, no theta.
meta.exit: atr_trail (chart-stop floor + chandelier trail, mult 2.5); hard EOD flat

concentration_fix.rationale: causal as-of VIX regime gate x day-trend-side alignment removes low-conviction catch-ups so the surviving edge is broad-based (drop-top5 positive), not a few monster days

gate_definitions.3: top-5 winning days < 200% of total P&L
gate_definitions.7: beats random-entry null (mean), same exit/count/side on GATED entries (L172)
gate_definitions.8: no-truncation: chart-stop+EOD does not flip a positive cell negative (L171)

symmetry_verdict: ASYMMETRIC (confirmed): MES-leads->trade-MNQ is the dominant catch-up side (max OOS $81.76/trade, best near-miss fails 1 gate(s)) vs MNQ-leads->trade-MES (max OOS $24.18/trade, fails 2 gate(s)). The OTHER lead direction does NOT have a stronger divergence asymmetry — it is the weaker, non-tradeable side. Neither side clears all 8 gates (both die on the SAME structural failure: tail concentration, gate 5).
rescue_verdict: NOT RESCUED: the concentration fix does not lift any MES->MNQ cell through all 8 gates (gate 5 concentration persists or another gate breaks under the gated population)
